title: Fixing Type Inference for a Generic Function with Overlapping Type Guards

statement.inputFormat: A type parameter `T` or an object of type `T`
statement.notes: The function should return the type string representation of `T` if it is assignable to `string` or `number`, and the type string representation of `T` if not.

hints.text: Check the condition for the type guard

editorial.title: Fixing Type Inference for a Generic Function with Overlapping Type Guards
editorial.intuition: The function should have a single type guard for `T` that checks if it is assignable to `string` or `number`.
editorial.bruteForce: The current implementation has overlapping type guards, which is incorrect.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to remove the second type guard and use a single type guard for `T`.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correct function should return the type string representation of `T` if it is assignable to `string` or `number`, and the type string representation of `T` if not.
